claim: The harness matters as much as the model: the ecosystem around Claude Code (global rules, skills, MCP servers, hooks) impacts effectiveness more than the underlying LLM quality.
actionable: Invest effort in building a comprehensive AI layer (Claude.md hierarchy, hooks, skills, LSP, MCP) for your codebase rather than assuming a better model alone will solve problems.
source_title: Anthropic Just Dropped a Masterclass on Building Agent Harnesses (for Large Codebases)

claim: Keep global rules lean and layered: avoid thousands-of-lines rule files that overwhelm the LLM; use layered CLAUDE.md files in subdirectories to progressively disclose conventions based on context.
actionable: Create minimal root CLAUDE.md with core info (tech stack, conventions, commands); add subdirectory-specific CLAUDE.md files that load automatically when navigating those areas.
source_title: Anthropic Just Dropped a Masterclass on Building Agent Harnesses (for Large Codebases)

claim: Hooks enable self-improving AI layers: stop hooks can reflect on session changes and propose CLAUDE.md updates while context is fresh; start hooks load team-specific context dynamically.
actionable: Set up end-of-session hooks that run Claude in headless mode to analyze changes and recommend CLAUDE.md updates; use start hooks to pull fresh documentation or context from external sources.
source_title: Anthropic Just Dropped a Masterclass on Building Agent Harnesses (for Large Codebases)

claim: Skills with scoped paths activate only in relevant directories: scope skills to specific paths (e.g., API route skill only in /api) to prevent overwhelming agents with unrelated conventions.
actionable: Use the path parameter in skill definitions to scope them to subdirectories; combine with layered CLAUDE.md to enforce single-purpose workflows in each code section.
source_title: Anthropic Just Dropped a Masterclass on Building Agent Harnesses (for Large Codebases)

claim: Language Server Protocol (LSP) via MCP enables symbol-level code search instead of string-based grep, providing better navigation and reducing token inefficiency in large codebases.
actionable: Build or integrate an MCP server that exposes LSP capabilities (go-to-definition, find-references) so agents can perform directed searches instead of grep.
source_title: Anthropic Just Dropped a Masterclass on Building Agent Harnesses (for Large Codebases)

claim: Sub-agents for exploration preserve primary agent context: delegate web research or code exploration to sub-agents so the main session doesn't bloat with hundreds of thousands of tokens before editing.
actionable: Dispatch research and analysis tasks to separate sub-agents; collect summaries and recommendations; return only the digest to the primary agent context.
source_title: Anthropic Just Dropped a Masterclass on Building Agent Harnesses (for Large Codebases)

claim: Assign ownership for AI layer adoption: have a champion or small team build the initial AI layer infrastructure, then roll it out to the organization for consistency rather than everyone building separate systems.
actionable: Designate an AI layer champion/team; invest time in building shared rules, skills, and tooling; distribute this standard across the organization to ensure consistent adoption.
source_title: Anthropic Just Dropped a Masterclass on Building Agent Harnesses (for Large Codebases)

claim: Claude Code navigates using genetic search (grep-like tools) rather than semantic search/indexing; this requires agents to have enough starting context to know where to look.
actionable: Provide clear codebase documentation and architecture overviews in your CLAUDE.md so agents can effectively use command-line tools for navigation.
source_title: Anthropic Just Dropped a Masterclass on Building Agent Harnesses (for Large Codebases)
